Metadata.description: Spectr builds a sovereign Norwegian ISR platform — autonomous, EW-resilient aerial systems and command software.

Home.missionStatement: Spectr builds autonomous systems that see and act on a comms-denied battlefield — sovereign hardware and software, developed and controlled from Norway.
Home.reconDescription: Long-range, autonomous ISR platform for continuous surveillance — without ground infrastructure or GPS dependency.
Home.centurionDescription: One sovereign command platform with AI-assisted decision support, multi-drone coordination, and mission staging.
Home.familyTitle: One integrated system. Hardware and software built together.
Home.norwayDescription: Spectr designs and produces ISR hardware and Centurion command software in Norway, with NATO-compatible allied components and no critical dependency on non-allied suppliers.

Products.title: Products
Products.heroDescription: RECON for autonomous ISR. CENTURION for sovereign mission command. Built and controlled from Norway.
Products.reconExtra: The operator defines the mission in Centurion. RECON takes off vertically, cruises autonomously, and returns — even when GPS and communications are down.
Products.specSheetDescription: Platform dimensions, performance targets, payload configuration, and operating profile for RECON.

About.title: About Us
About.heroIntro: Spectr is a Norwegian defense technology company developing autonomous unmanned systems, mission software, and sovereign avionics for demanding operational environments.

overview.title: Company Overview
paragraphs: spectr as is a Norwegian defense technology company headquartered in Norway (Org.nr 936 961 967). We develop unmanned aerial systems, autonomous defense software, and onboard avionics for intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance in contested environments.

Our work spans hardware and software: from airframe design and flight controllers to mission command systems and AI-assisted decision support. Everything is built with a focus on field readiness, sovereign control, and operational reliability outside the lab.

mission.title: Mission
paragraphs: Spectr builds autonomous systems that see and act on a comms-denied battlefield. We believe allied forces need ISR capability they can trust, control, and sustain — without dependence on foreign supply chains for critical components.

Our mission is to deliver sovereign Norwegian hardware and software that operates when GPS is denied, communications are jammed, and conventional systems fail. We build for the conditions operators actually face.

whatWeBuild.title: What We Build
paragraphs: RECON is our VTOL fixed-wing ISR platform for continuous surveillance over large areas. It executes autonomous missions in GNSS-denied and comms-denied environments. The prototype is assembled with ground testing ongoing, advancing toward flight test and operational demonstration.

CENTURION is our sovereign mission command platform. The operator defines goals and waypoints; Centurion plans, executes, and reports. Version 0.1 is operational with mission staging, AI-assisted decision support, and multi-drone coordination.

Beyond platforms, we design and produce avionics in-house: flight controllers, onboard computers, telemetry radios, and battery packs with BMS and thermal management. Airframes are built in vacuum-formed carbon fiber composite. Final assembly and software integration are completed in Norway.

whyNorway.title: Why Norway
paragraphs: Norway provides a stable, NATO-aligned foundation for developing critical defense technology under national control. Spectr produces in Norway to maintain sovereignty over design, manufacturing, and software — reducing supply-chain risk and ensuring allied compatibility.

We use allied suppliers only where necessary: EO/IR gimbals, battery cells, and motors. Everything else — airframes, avionics, flight controllers, onboard computers, telemetry, battery pack design, Centurion software, and final assembly — is developed and produced in-house in Norway.
This model delivers capable systems at a fraction of the cost of today's alternatives, while keeping critical technology local and NDAA Section 889 compatible by design.

technology.title: Technology & Innovation
paragraphs: RECON is designed for resilient operation in denied environments. Our navigation stack includes Visual-Inertial Odometry in development, terrain-referenced navigation on the roadmap, and tactical-grade INS as fallback. Multi-layer positioning and resilient communications keep the platform operating when conventional links fail.

On the hardware side, we engineer power-dense battery packs, custom flight controllers, and secure onboard systems aligned with NATO STANAG principles. Carbon fiber composite airframes are vacuum-formed for strength-to-weight performance suited to extended ISR missions.
CENTURION brings AI-assisted decision support and natural language interfaces to mission command — helping operators maintain context and coordinate multiple assets under pressure.

security.title: Security & Compliance
paragraphs: Spectr operates in a sensitive sector. Our products, software, and technical information may be subject to Norwegian export controls, EU export control regulations, and international sanctions laws. We conduct qualification review before engaging with customers and may decline transactions where legal or national-security concerns arise.

We maintain Norwegian ownership and controlled infrastructure. Customer data, mission information, and proprietary technology are handled under strict confidentiality. Defense-related inquiries should not include classified material through public channels.
For more detail on our security commitments, see our

leadership.title: Leadership
paragraphs: Spectr is led by Makwan Soran, Chief Executive Officer. The company is built by engineers working across aerospace hardware, embedded systems, and operational software — with a shared focus on systems that perform in real-world conditions.
We are a small, execution-focused team. We hire for practical engineering ability, disciplined delivery, and the judgment required to build technology for demanding operational environments.

Careers.title: Join Our Mission
Careers.intro: Spectr is building mission-ready aerospace systems and operational software from Norway.
Careers.paragraph1: We are looking for people who care about practical engineering, disciplined execution, and technology that can hold up outside the lab.
Careers.paragraph2: If you want to work across aerospace hardware, operator workflow, and secure software systems, we want to hear from you.

Security.title: Security
Security.description: Spectr security, confidentiality, Norwegian ownership, and controlled infrastructure commitments.
Security.legalText1: Our operations are structured in accordance with the Norwegian Security Act (sikkerhetsloven) and the Business Security Regulation (virksomhetsikkerhetsforskriften § 83). We maintain organisational and technical safeguards for classified information and critical infrastructure under Norwegian law.
Security.legalText2: We believe critical technology should be developed, owned, and controlled locally — to safeguard Norwegian interests for the long term.
Security.nationalControl: We believe critical technology should be developed under full national control.

Investor.title: Investor Relations
Investor.intro: Follow Spectr's company updates, operating milestones, and long-term aerospace systems roadmap.
Investor.headline: Sovereign ISR capability from Norway.
Investor.paragraph1: Spectr is developing RECON — an autonomous, EW-resilient ISR platform — and Centurion, a sovereign command platform for multi-drone mission staging and AI-assisted decision support.
Investor.paragraph2: Investor relations inquiries are handled directly by the Spectr team. We share company materials, milestone updates, and strategic context with qualified stakeholders.
Investor.quote: The West needs ISR drones that see everything — and cannot be knocked out.
